prior_state_v1.02: vk_compress_one / vk_decompress_one routed through CPU staged VHT2; GPU dispatch guessed to hit VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST at first vkWaitForFences on RTX 2060
v1.03_finding: GPU dispatch runs to completion without VK_ERROR_DEVICE_LOST — the crash hypothesis is no longer reproducing. The 4-shader pipeline (vilenkin → mobius → band_quantize → band_dequantize) completes and returns data. Correlation is wrong but not zero.
interpretation: Shader semantics drift vs CPU reference — output is correlated with input (not random) but substantially wrong. Likely culprits (need RenderDoc + instrumented shader to isolate): (a) 1/sqrt(p) per-stage normalisation mismatch inside vilenkin.comp; (b) Möbius gather/scatter ordering; (c) per-band amax reduction in band_quantize.comp not matching the CPU reference's layout. None of these are the DEVICE_LOST fear the comment blocks predicted.
next_actions: Instrument vilenkin.comp with a single-stage output dump and compare against sp_vht2_forward_f32 byte-for-byte on a known input.
Register VK_EXT_debug_utils callback so the Khronos validation layer's findings reach stderr instead of the VK debug channel.
Once shader parity is achieved, flip the default in vk_compress_one to force_gpu=1.

files_changed.backends/vulkan/shannon_prime_vulkan.c: added SHANNON_PRIME_VULKAN_FORCE_GPU=1 env gate on vk_compress_one + vk_decompress_one so the GPU dispatch can be exercised without disabling the default CPU fallback
